name: Coveralls Code Coverage API
what_it_does: Code coverage tracking and reporting service with REST API for submitting coverage reports from CI/CD pipelines, querying coverage history, and managing coverage badges for open-source and private repositories.
use_cases: Submitting code coverage reports from CI/CD pipelines after test runs
Querying coverage trends and history for automated regression detection agents
Managing per-repository coverage requirements and blocking PRs below threshold
Generating coverage badge embeds for documentation and README automation
Correlating test coverage changes with pull requests in code review automation
not_for: Full test management and result tracking (use TestRail or similar)
best_when: An agent integrates with CI/CD to track code coverage trends, enforce coverage thresholds, and alert on coverage regressions.
avoid_when: You need advanced code quality metrics beyond coverage — use SonarCloud or DeepSource.

auth.notes: Repository token for coverage submission (from Coveralls settings). GitHub/GitLab OAuth for account-level operations. Token per repository for CI integration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for open-source repositories; unlimited coverage submissions
pricing.paid_tiers: Team from $10/month for private repos; Business from $50/month
pricing.notes: Free for open-source projects — widely used in the OSS community. Paid plans for private repositories. Simple pricing based on private repo count.

performance.rate_limits: Not documented; designed for CI/CD submission patterns

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is basic. Coverage submission is the primary API use case. Query APIs for badges and coverage data exist but are lightly documented. CLI tools (coveralls-ruby, coveralls-python) handle most CI integration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Coverage submissions for same commit are deduplicated. Re-submitting same coverage data is safe.
known_agent_gotchas: Coverage format must match expectations — LCOV, SimpleCov, Istanbul formats supported
CI environment variables (COVERALLS_REPO_TOKEN) must be configured for each repo
API documentation is minimal — most integrations use official language libraries, not raw REST
Historical data access via API is limited — dashboards show trends but API query is restricted

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GitHub OAuth for auth. Repository token per project limits scope. No advanced security features — basic service focused on coverage tracking.
